title: Cnidoscolus oligandrus
intro: Cnidoscolus oligandrus, commonly known as the Mexican sand box tree or Mexican sand box, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cnidoscolus oligandrus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cnidoscolus oligandrus is often prepared as a decoction or infused in tea, or used in topical creams and ointments to take advantage of its health benefits.
taxonomy: Cnidoscolus oligandrus belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae. The species is native to South America and is part of the genus Cnidoscolus. It is a type of cactus-like plant with a diverse range of classifications. The plant is sometimes classified as a species of the genus Jatropha due to its similar appearance. However, it is primarily classified within the Euphorbiaceae family.

side_effects: Cnidoscolus oligandrus is not a well-documented plant, but based on its close relatives, Cnidoscolus chinensis and Cnidoscolus grandiflorus, which are part of the same genus, I can provide some general information. Cnidoscolus oligandrus may cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals due to the presence of urushiol, a toxic compound found in its sap. Ingesting the plant may also lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The sap of Cnidoscolus oligandrus may cause blistering, burning, and redness on the skin, similar to that of poison ivy or poison oak. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of its potential side effects.
